Breast cancer Literature Review

Bonface Onyango

2026-04-14

Setup and Libraries

Activate r_env

  • Libraries installed in virtual r_env
  • conda activate r_env
# Core libraries 
library(dplyr) 
library(ggplot2) 
library(igraph) 
library(ggraph) 
library(readr)
library(devtools)

Install lisearch

#uncomment or install in r env
#library(devtools)      # now should work
#install_github("elizagrames/litsearchr", ref="main")
library(litsearchr)

Litsearch version

packageVersion("litsearchr")
[1] ‘1.0.0’

Search query

  • https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/advanced/

  • ​“breast cancer” AND (Algeria OR Angola OR Benin OR Botswana OR “Burkina Faso” OR Burundi OR “Cape Verde” OR Cameroon OR “Central African Republic” OR Chad OR Comoros OR “Democratic Republic of the Congo” OR “Republic of the Congo” OR Djibouti OR Egypt OR “Equatorial Guinea” OR Eritrea OR Eswatini OR Ethiopia OR Gabon OR Gambia OR Ghana OR Guinea OR “Guinea-Bissau” OR “Ivory Coast” OR Kenya OR Lesotho OR Liberia OR Libya OR Madagascar OR Malawi OR Mali OR Mauritania OR Mauritius OR Morocco OR Mozambique OR Namibia OR Niger OR Nigeria OR Rwanda OR “Sao Tome and Principe” OR Senegal OR Seychelles OR “Sierra Leone” OR Somalia OR “South Africa” OR “South Sudan” OR Sudan OR Tanzania OR Togo OR Tunisia OR Uganda OR Zambia OR Zimbabwe) AND (Europe* OR *America* OR Australia* ) AND (genetics OR environment OR healthcare) AND (incidence OR gene* OR clinical* OR treatment* OR and survival*)

Loading and inspection of results

df_pubmed<-import_results(file="../papers/pubmed-breastcanc-set.nbib")
Reading file ../papers/pubmed-breastcanc-set.nbib ... done
df_pubmed <- as.data.frame(df_pubmed)
head(df_pubmed)

Number of articles -> 394

nrow(df_pubmed)
[1] 394
#We can take a look at the first few.
df_pubmed[1:3,]

Extract keywords->tagged

extract_terms(keywords=df_pubmed[, "keywords"], method="tagged")

Extract keywords -> cleaned

  • The keywords had new lines and we had to reformat it before processding
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)

clean_keywords <- function(x) {
  # Handle NULL or empty
  if (is.null(x) || length(x) == 0) return(character(0))
  
  # Remove NA
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(character(0))
  
  # Clean and split
  x %>%
    str_replace_all("\\n", " ") %>%     # remove newlines
    str_replace_all("\"", "") %>%       # remove quotes
    str_trim() %>%                      # trim whitespace
    str_split(",") %>%                  # split by comma
    unlist() %>%                        # flatten
    str_trim() %>%                      # trim again
    discard(~ .x %in% c("", "NULL", "character0"))
}

# Apply to df_pubmed$keywords and flatten
df_pubmed <- df_pubmed %>%
  mutate(keywords_clean = map(keywords, clean_keywords))

# Flatten all keywords
keywords_flat <- df_pubmed$keywords_clean %>%
  flatten_chr()

# Remove empty
keywords_flat <- keywords_flat[keywords_flat != ""]
keywords_flat[1:5]
[1] "BRCA1"         "BRCA2"         "breast cancer" "ethnicity"     "geography"    
# Preview

Tagged keywords

keywords_extracted <- extract_terms(
  keywords = keywords_flat,
  method = "tagged"
)

# --- Step 4: Preview ---
keywords_extracted[1:20]

)

all_stopwords <- c(get_stopwords("English"), clinpsy_stopwords)
print(length(all_stopwords))
[1] 698
head(all_stopwords, 10)
 [1] "a"           "a's"         "able"        "about"       "above"       "according"  
 [7] "accordingly" "across"      "actually"    "after"

Keywords from tittle after removing stop words ->78

title_terms <- extract_terms(
text=df_pubmed[, "title"],
method="fakerake",
min_freq=3, min_n=2,
stopwords=all_stopwords
)
print(length(title_terms))
[1] 78
head(title_terms,10)
 [1] "advanced breast"         "advanced breast cancer"  "african american"       
 [4] "african ancestry"        "african breast"          "african women"          
 [7] "biomarker working"       "brca2 mutation"          "brca2 mutation carriers"
[10] "brca2 mutations"

Keywords + title terms ->181

terms <- unique(c(keywords_extracted_min_1, title_terms))
print(length(terms))
[1] 181
head(terms,10)
 [1] "3-f"                    "adjuvant therapy"       "advanced breast cancer"
 [4] "africa"                 "african"                "african ancestry"      
 [7] "apoptosis"              "bi-rads"                "biomarker"             
[10] "brca"

Network analysis

docs <- paste(df_pubmed[, "title"], df_pubmed[, "keywords"])
docs[4]
[1] "Fine-mapping of 150 breast cancer risk regions identifies 191 likely target genes. NULL"
dfm <- create_dfm(elements=docs, features=terms)
dfm[1:5, 1:7]
     3-f adjuvant therapy advanced breast cancer africa african african ancestry apoptosis
[1,]   0                0                      0      0       0                0         0
[2,]   0                0                      0      0       0                0         0
[3,]   0                0                      0      1       1                0         0
[4,]   0                0                      0      0       0                0         1
[5,]   0                0                      0      0       0                0         0
g <- create_network(dfm, min_studies=3)
ggraph(g, layout="stress") +
coord_fixed() +
expand_limits(x=c(-3, 3)) +
geom_edge_link(aes(alpha=weight)) +
geom_node_point(shape="circle filled", fill="white") +
geom_node_text(aes(label=name), hjust="outward", check_overlap=TRUE) +
guides(edge_alpha=FALSE)

strengths <- strength(g)
data.frame(term=names(strengths), strength=strengths, row.names=NULL) %>%
mutate(rank=rank(strength, ties.method="min")) %>%
arrange(strength) ->
term_strengths
term_strengths
cutoff_fig <- ggplot(term_strengths, aes(x=rank, y=strength, label=term)) +
geom_line() +
geom_point() +
geom_text(data=filter(term_strengths, rank>5), hjust="right", nudge_y=20,
check_overlap=TRUE)
cutoff_fig

cutoff_cum <- find_cutoff(g, method="cumulative", percent=0.8)
cutoff_cum
[1] 181
cutoff_fig +
geom_hline(yintercept=cutoff_cum, linetype="dashed")

get_keywords(reduce_graph(g, cutoff_cum))
 [1] "africa"                        "african"                       "african ancestry"              "biomarker"                     "brca"                          "brca1"                        
 [7] "brca2"                         "breast"                        "breast cancer"                 "bse"                           "cancer"                        "cervical cancer"

[43] "women"                         "women of african ancestry"     "african american"              "brca2 mutation"                "breast cancer screening"       "breast cancers"               
[49] "cancer incidence"              "cancer screening"              "cancer susceptibility"         "european ancestry"             "genetic variants"              "genome-wide association"      
[55] "mutation carriers"             "national cancer"               "negative breast"               "negative breast cancer"        "triple-negative breast"       
cutoff_change <- find_cutoff(g, method="changepoint", knot_num=3)
cutoff_change
[1]  387  855 1378 3447
cutoff_fig +
geom_hline(yintercept=cutoff_change, linetype="dashed")

g_redux <- reduce_graph(g, cutoff_change[1])
selected_terms <- get_keywords(g_redux)
selected_terms
 [1] "africa"                 "african"                "brca"                   "brca1"                  "brca2"                  "breast"                 "breast cancer"          "bse"                   
 [9] "cancer"                 "her2"                   "incidence"              "intervention"           "knowledge"              "mutation"               "practice"               "prevention"            
[17] "risk factors"           "screening"              "survey"                 "women"                  "cancer screening"       "negative breast"        "negative breast cancer"
extra_terms <- c(
"medication",
"cognitive-behavioural therapy",
"cognitive behavioural therapy"
)
selected_terms <- c(selected_terms, extra_terms)
selected_terms
 [1] "africa"                        "african"                       "brca"                          "brca1"                         "brca2"                         "breast"
